seo.title: AI Agent Error Handling Patterns in Google ADK Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent error handling patterns in Google ADK agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Google ADK, AI Agent Error Handling Patterns manifest as cascading failures in multi-agent workflows where unhandled errors in tool calls, delegation loops, and model inconsistencies disrupt session state and produce unreliable outputs. Common issues arise from Gemini model switching between agents, leading to format mismatches, and circular delegation in agent hierarchies that exhaust resources without resolution. These patterns are exacerbated by ADK's session-based state management, where long conversations corrupt shared context without robust error boundaries.

howItManifests.symptom: Agents endlessly delegate tasks between each other without progress, consuming API quota
howItManifests.impact: Infinite loops crash workflows, exhaust Gemini rate limits, and return timeout errors

howItManifests.scenario: Gemini model switching causing output inconsistency
howItManifests.symptom: Different agents using gemini-1.5-pro vs gemini-1.5-flash produce incompatible JSON schemas
howItManifests.impact: Tool response parsing fails across agent boundaries, breaking workflow execution

howItManifests.scenario: Session state corruption in long conversations
howItManifests.symptom: Shared session.state accumulates malformed tool responses from failed agent calls
howItManifests.impact: Subsequent agent turns receive corrupted context, producing hallucinated or irrelevant responses

howItManifests.scenario: Tool response format mismatches across agents
howItManifests.symptom: One agent returns plain text while another expects structured JSON tool results
howItManifests.impact: Downstream agents fail to parse responses, causing workflow orchestration to halt

detection.name: Circular Delegation Detector
detection.description: Monitors agent invocation traces for repeated delegation patterns exceeding threshold

detection.name: Model Consistency Checker
detection.description: Validates all agents in workflow use identical Gemini model versions

detection.name: Session State Validator
detection.description: Scans session state for corrupted tool response accumulations
detection.code:
def validate_session_state(session):
    state = session.state
    tool_responses = state.get('tool_responses', [])
    
    corrupted = 0
    for response in tool_responses[-20:]:  # Last 20 responses
        if not isinstance(response, dict) or 'content' not in response:
            corrupted += 1
            print(f'Corrupted tool response: {response}')
    
    if corrupted > 3:
        raise Exception(f'Session state corrupted: {corrupted}/20 malformed tool responses')
    
    print(f'✅ Session state healthy: {corrupted} minor issues')

fixes.name: Delegation Circuit Breaker
fixes.description: Implements max-delegation-depth limit with fallback routing
fixes.code:
from google_adk import Agent, before_agent_callback

@before_agent_callback
def delegation_circuit_breaker(ctx, agent):
    session = ctx.session
    delegation_count = session.state.get('delegation_depth', 0)
    
    if delegation_count > 5:
        # Fallback to root agent
        ctx.redirect_to('root_agent')
        session.state['delegation_depth'] = 0
        return
    
    session.state['delegation_depth'] = delegation_count + 1

fixes.name: Unified Response Normalizer
fixes.description: Standardizes tool responses across all agents to consistent JSON schema

fixes.name: State Reset on Critical Error
fixes.description: Resets corrupted session state while preserving conversation history

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Error Handling Patterns Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates seamlessly as a before_agent_callback in Google ADK workflows, automatically detecting circular delegations, model inconsistencies, and state corruption in real-time. It captures agent traces, validates response schemas across the multi-agent hierarchy, and enforces delegation depth limits without modifying core agent logic. When issues are detected, Vex can trigger automatic remediation like state resets or fallback routing while generating detailed observability reports.

The Vex SDK provides production-grade error boundaries that wrap ADK's native execution, catching failures that would otherwise cascade through agent delegation chains. Configuration is simple - just add the Vex callback and define your error thresholds. Vex's ML-powered anomaly detection learns your workflow's normal behavior and flags deviations before they impact users.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk import Vex
from google_adk import Agent, Workflow, before_agent_callback

# Install: pip install google-adk vex-sdk
vex = Vex(api_key='your-vex-api-key')

@vex.monitor_errors(
    patterns=['circular_delegation', 'model_inconsistency', 'state_corruption'],
    max_delegations=5,
    auto_remediate=True
)
@before_agent_callback
def vex_adk_guardrail(ctx, agent):
    # Vex automatically detects:
    # 1. Circular delegation loops
    # 2. Gemini model version mismatches
    # 3. Malformed tool responses
    # 4. Session state bloat
    
    issues = vex.scan_session(ctx.session_id)
    if issues:
        vex.log_alert(ctx.session_id, issues)
        if issues.get('circular_delegation'):
            ctx.redirect_to('fallback_agent')
        
# Usage in your ADK workflow
workflow = Workflow(agents={
    'root': root_agent,
    'research': research_agent,
    'analyzer': analyzer_agent
}, callbacks=[vex_adk_guardrail])

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add Vex to your Google ADK workflows in 3 lines of code to eliminate agent error cascades forever.
